title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: Reviewer flags that the new Pass-0 adoption wait regresses CC-0079's exactly-once event semantics: because the PushSecret stays non-Terminating during adoption, `FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets` now fires on every 15s requeue instead of once per termination (`keystone_controller.go:369-381`). They want the emit gated on Pass-0 being cleared (either moved into `finalizeOpenBaoSecrets` right before Pass-1, or by treating unadopted PushSecrets as already-Terminating for emission), plus an integration-test assertion in `TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption` stage 1 capping the event count at ≤1 across the adoption-wait window.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 889e22e with Claude Code

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=0 critical=0 warning=2 info=3 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## WARNING (2)

### W-001: FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets event now fires on every requeue during Pass-0 adoption wait (regresses CC-0079 exactly-once semantics)
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:369-381` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The pre-existing exactly-once semantics per termination relied on Pass-1 immediately flipping the PushSecret to Terminating so hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets would return false on subsequent requeues. Pass-0 breaks that invariant: while waiting for ESO adoption, the PushSecret stays non-Terminating, so hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets returns true on every 15s RequeueSecretPolling requeue and the reconciler keeps emitting the FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets Normal Event. Because SetupWithManager does not watch PushSecret, this is a steady ~1-event-per-15s stream until ESO adopts — the opposite of the CC-0079 exactly-once guarantee documented by the nearby comment.

**Code**:
```
	hasLiveCleanupWork, err := r.hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets(ctx, keystone)
	if err != nil {
		return ctrl.Result{}, err
	}
	if hasLiveCleanupWork {
		r.Recorder.Event(keystone, corev1.EventTypeNormal, "FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets",
			"Cleaning up OpenBao backup PushSecrets before removing Keystone")
	}
```

**Action Required**: Gate the FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets emit on Pass-0 being cleared: either move the emit into finalizeOpenBaoSecrets right before Pass-1, or teach hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets to treat unadopted PushSecrets like already-Terminating for emission purposes. Add an integration-test assertion in TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption stage 1 that counts FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets events and expects ≤ 1 across the adoption-wait window.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Compute adoption state in the same pass used for hasLiveCleanupWork and suppress the FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets emit while any backup PushSecret is still unadopted (Pass-0 not yet cleared). Either fold the adoption check into hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets so it returns false while adoption is pending, or relocate the Recorder.Event call into finalizeOpenBaoSecrets immediately before Pass-1 fires. Also add an integration-test assertion counting FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets events in TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption stage 1 with an upper bound of 1.
```

**Related**: I-001

---

### W-002: Misleading doc comment on addESOAdoptionFinalizerToPushSecret test helper
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go:2936-2950` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The helper's doc comment claims that client.Delete on a PushSecret holding only esoPushSecretFinalizer will garbage-collect the object immediately rather than flip it into Terminating. That is false — both the controller-runtime fake client and real Kubernetes treat any non-empty metadata.finalizers as blocking; the specific finalizer string is not interpreted. The test still passes because teardown calls clearESOFinalizerFromPushSecret, but the comment will mislead future maintainers who copy-paste the helper expecting immediate GC.

**Code**:
```
// addESOAdoptionFinalizerToPushSecret attaches only esoPushSecretFinalizer —
// the adoption signal. Without esoCleanupFinalizer, a client.Delete on the
// PushSecret will garbage-collect it immediately rather than flipping it into
// Terminating. Use this helper to simulate ESO adopting a PushSecret that is
// not expected to hold up deletion — e.g. credential-keys-backup in the
// OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked test, which must satisfy Pass-0 without also
// simulating a stuck kv-v2 purge (CC-0091, REQ-001, REQ-003, REQ-007).
```

**Action Required**: Rewrite the comment to describe what the helper actually models: a PushSecret that ESO has adopted (Pass-0 passes) but whose Delete flow is not being used to simulate a slow remote DeleteSecret. Make clear that any finalizer still blocks etcd removal.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
// addESOAdoptionFinalizerToPushSecret attaches only esoPushSecretFinalizer —
// the adoption signal. This simulates the narrow window after ESO has
// adopted a PushSecret (first reconcile has installed the adoption
// finalizer) but before a client.Delete has fired. Any finalizer still
// blocks etcd removal, so a subsequent Delete flips the object into
// Terminating exactly as it does with addESOFinalizerToPushSecret — use
// this helper when the test only needs to satisfy Pass-0 and does not
// care about modelling ESO's kv-v2 purge latency (CC-0091, REQ-001,
// REQ-003, REQ-007).
```

---

## INFO (3)

### I-001: Controller does not watch PushSecret, so Pass-0 adoption wait adds up to 15s of deletion latency
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:567-604` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: EnsurePushSecret (internal/common/secrets/secrets.go:133) sets a controller reference so Keystone owns PushSecret, but SetupWithManager does not declare Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}). When ESO installs its adoption finalizer on the PushSecret, the controller does not wake up and must wait for the next RequeueSecretPolling (15s) tick before advancing past Pass-0. Correctness is unaffected but deletion takes up to 15s longer per unadopted PushSecret on the first reconcile after a fast-deleted CR.

**Code**:
```
		Owns(&batchv1.CronJob{}).
```

**Action Required**: Add Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}) to the builder chain in SetupWithManager so the adoption-wait latency collapses from up to 15s to watch-event delivery latency (single-digit ms). Safe as a one-liner follow-up PR to keep this fix minimal.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
		Owns(&batchv1.CronJob{}).
		Owns(&esov1alpha1.PushSecret{}).
```

**Related**: W-001

---

### I-002: Pass-0 Get is redundant with Pass-2 Get on the happy path
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go:207-270` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: Each reconcile now issues two Get calls per backup PushSecret (one in Pass-0 at lines 207-231, one in Pass-2 at lines 253-270). On the common happy path — both PushSecrets adopted, Pass-1 fires Delete, Pass-2 still observes Terminating — this is 4 Gets + 2 Deletes per reconcile, double the pre-CC-0091 API call count. Minor cluster API-server load cost, not a correctness issue, but worth noting for throughput planning on clusters with many Keystone CRs churning.

**Code**:
```
Pass-0 Get at reconcile_secrets.go:207-231 and Pass-2 Get at reconcile_secrets.go:253-270 both fetch the same PushSecret per name.
```

**Action Required**: Consider having Pass-0 cache the fetched PushSecret object state into a local map keyed by name so Pass-2 can skip the re-Get on the happy path. This is arguably over-engineering for a 2-element iteration, so defer unless cluster-wide throughput becomes a concern.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Introduce a small intra-reconcile cache (local map[string]*esov1alpha1.PushSecret) populated in Pass-0 and consulted in Pass-2, with fallthrough to Get on cache miss. Only apply if throughput profiling later justifies it — the 2-element loop makes this premature optimization today.
```

---

### I-003: TestFinalizeOpenBaoSecrets_MixedAdoptionState uses BeNumerically(">=", 2) where Equal(2) is expected
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets_test.go:1299-1300` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The assertion BeNumerically(">=", 2) is looser than the contract requires. Pass-1 iterates over a bounded 2-name list and must issue exactly one Delete per adopted PushSecret once Pass-0 clears — a future regression that accidentally issues an extra Delete (e.g., a retry inside a helper) would slip past this assertion unnoticed.

**Code**:
```
	g.Expect(deleteCount).To(BeNumerically(">=", 2), ...)
```

**Action Required**: Tighten the assertion to Equal(2) so that any extra Delete call is caught.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
	g.Expect(deleteCount).To(Equal(2),
		"Pass-1 must fire Delete exactly once on every adopted PushSecret once the Pass-0 gate opens")
```

---

## Summary

A well-scoped, well-motivated fix for the CC-0091 delete race: the three-pass design is clean, the symmetric fernet/credential coverage via a single openBaoBackupPushSecretNames loop is consistent with the existing CC-0079 pattern, and the WaitingForESOAdoption vs. OpenBaoFinalizerBlocked split gives SREs the right signal. Test coverage is unusually thorough (unit tests per branch plus a full envtest integration test across all three lifecycle stages) and the docs update accurately references the motivating CI run. The main concern is that the CC-0079 exactly-once event-emission contract was not reconsidered for Pass-0 and now regresses to a ~1-event-per-15s stream during the very race this PR targets; the misleading adoption-finalizer helper comment is a smaller but real documentation defect. Remaining findings (missing PushSecret watch, redundant Get, loose numeric assertion) are follow-up opportunities rather than blockers.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| WARNING  | 2 |
| INFO     | 3 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Address Finding 1 (event-emission regression) and Finding 2 (misleading helper comment) before merging. The INFO findings (missing PushSecret watch, redundant Get, tightening the deleteCount assertion) can land as follow-up PRs and should not block this change.
resolution_summary: Addressed W-001 by gating FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets emission on ESO adoption state. `hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets` in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:419 now also skips PushSecrets that lack ESO's cleanup finalizer (via the new `hasESOFinalizer` helper), treating unadopted PushSecrets like already-Terminating for emission purposes, so Pass-0 adoption waits no longer produce one event per 15s requeue. The in-file comment at keystone_controller.go:370-378 was rewritten to document that the gate now covers Pass-0 as well, and the sibling doc comment on `hasLiveOpenBaoBackupPushSecrets` was extended to list the three disqualifiers (NotFound, Terminating, Adopted=false). A stage-1 assertion was added in integration_test.go:3304-3323 of `TestIntegrationKeystone_DeleteRacingESOAdoption` that lists Events on the namespace, filters by InvolvedObject.UID and Reason==FinalizingOpenBaoSecrets across the adoption-wait window, and requires the count to be ≤ 1 — exactly as the reviewer requested.
